matches.reading: Seattle shakes — the hosts one win from a first quarter-final since 1930, against the empire that broke them in 2014. The ghosts return: Pulisic scores where Wondolowski missed, and for an hour the dream is real. But De Bruyne's last dance has one more waltz; his free kick in the 88th, under Lumen's roar, is the cruelest goal of the tournament. Belgium, again.
matches.keyFactor: Belgium's big-game scar tissue — they've ended this exact American dream before

matches.reading: The golden children of the Bosphorus have charmed the orb all summer — but Atlanta is where fairytales meet the world champions. Güler will score against his idol's heirs, a goal he'll tell grandchildren about. The rest is Messi: a free kick, an assist conjured from dead air, and the machine of Lusail rolling into a fifth straight quarter-final.
matches.keyFactor: Argentina's settled core against knockout debutants — class gaps widen in July

matches.reading: Vancouver, and the Elephants — slayers of the maple press — charge at Ronaldo's last crusade with the most athletic front line left in the draw. Pépé scores early and BC Place believes in chaos. But Vitinha and Bruno Fernandes are too deep, too cold, too rehearsed: a Mendes thunderbolt levels it and Ronaldo's header, his fourth of the summer at 41, ends Africa's bravest run.
matches.keyFactor: Portugal's midfield class survives the athletic storm

matches.reading: Foxborough hosts a rematch of Euro qualifying's old rhythm: the Dutch, patched and proud, against the tournament's most complete predator. The orb shows Van Dijk heroic for an hour — then Dembélé, the Ballon d'Or carrying its own gravity, bends the night. Mbappé's second from the spot. Deschamps' farewell reaches a fourth consecutive semi-final; the Dutch curse holds.
matches.keyFactor: Netherlands' depleted depth finally runs out against France's bench

matches.reading: SoFi's silver basin, and the orb is almost embarrassed by its clarity: the golden generation's last stand against the dynasty in full bloom. De Bruyne conjures one final masterpiece for Doku — a goal of pure 2018 — but Spain score three because Spain always score three when the pitch is fast and the night is warm. The old kings bow; the new ones barely notice.
matches.keyFactor: A decade of Belgian wear against the youngest elite midfield alive

matches.reading: Miami, and the duel the planet demanded: Haaland against his kingdom's own league, Kane against the storm that buried Brazil. The orb shows Haaland scoring — nothing in this summer stops that — but England's iron, nine clean sheets deep, bends without breaking. Kane equalises from the spot; Bellingham, inevitable in July, sends the north wind home in the 89th. The Viking summer ends in glory and grief.
matches.keyFactor: England's defensive machine concedes once, never twice; Norway needs twice

matches.reading: Kansas City. Messi against Ronaldo — the last time, the very last time, two decades of religion compressed into ninety minutes. The orb refuses to look away: Ronaldo scores first, 41 years old, impossible, eternal. Then the universe corrects itself — Álvarez levels, and in the 87th Messi curls the goal that ends the argument forever. Football's longest sentence gets its full stop.
matches.keyFactor: Argentina's collective machine vs Portugal's reliance on moments — and destiny

matches.reading: Arlington holds the true final, one round early: the two best sides on earth, the old master Deschamps against the dynasty he can see coming. Mbappé rages gloriously against the dying of the light and scores the goal of the tournament — but Spain pass through the storm as if it were weather for other people. Yamal equalises; Olmo, from the bench, breaks French hearts in the 81st. Pozzo's ghost keeps his record.
matches.keyFactor: Spain's midfield denies France the transitions their whole game is built on

matches.reading: Atlanta, and sixty years of hurt against the champions of everything. The orb shows the tightest match of the tournament: Rice on Messi, Kane against Otamendi, an hour of pure chess. Kane scores; Álvarez answers within four minutes. And then, deep in added time, with penalties looming like an old English nightmare, Messi plays a pass that didn't exist until he imagined it — Lautaro, offside in every universe but this one, wins it. Hurt, renewed.
matches.keyFactor: Argentina's tournament nerve in the moments England have historically failed

matches.reading: Miami hosts the match nobody wants and the neutrals adore. Deschamps' final ninety minutes in the dugout; Tuchel facing his old Bundesliga shadow. The kids play — Doué, Zaïre-Émery, Gordon, Quansah — and Mbappé, chasing the Golden Boot's last goals, scores twice to claim it. Bronze for the old master's farewell; England fly home with familiar lessons.
matches.keyFactor: Mbappé's Golden Boot hunt gives the dead rubber a living pulse

matches.venue: MetLife Stadium, East Rutherford (New York/New Jersey)
matches.reading: MetLife, July 19. The dynasty against the dying of the greatest light: Spain's eternal midfield against Messi's final game for the only prize he could still want twice. The orb has never glowed brighter — Messi scores, of course he scores, a first-half free kick that makes time stop. But the future is patient: Yamal levels before the hour with the arrogance of seventeen... eighteen... whatever age genius is now, and Pedri's winner in the 74th is four hundred passes old. Spain, champions of the world — the second star, the dynasty confirmed, in the city where the orb first foresaw it.
matches.keyFactor: The best midfield on earth against the best story — and midfields win World Cups
